title: BitSight Findings API - Entity And Risk Vector Conventions
applies_to: BitSight findings retrieval by company and risk vector

confirmed_facts.fact: BitSight exposes findings by company entity under endpoints shaped like GET /v1/companies/{entity_guid}/findings with filters such as risk_vector.
The findings surface is organized around risk vectors, and documentation for specific finding classes maps directly to concrete API request patterns.
BitSight error handling uses standard HTTP status codes, including 401 when no valid API token is provided and 403 when the caller lacks permission to the resource.
Current ratings retrieval is exposed under a versioned ratings API path such as /ratings/v1/current-ratings, which indicates BitSight uses separate endpoint families for different data domains.

implementation_notes: Model findings ingestion around entity GUID plus risk vector filters, then normalize different findings classes in your own schema.
Handle permissions errors distinctly from missing-token errors because they signal different operational fixes.
Do not assume findings and ratings share identical paths or response shapes just because they are both in BitSight.
caveats: BitSight documents many finding types separately, so additional risk-vector-specific entries may still be worth adding later.
Access to some findings views is product- and role-dependent.
